news_body:
Mumbai: For the next three seasons, Indian Premier League (IPL) has carved out its biggest window ever. 
It will run for 72 days next year, up from 66 in 2024, and stretch to 78 days for in 2026 and 2027. By keeping for itself a quarter of the calendar year – including pre-tournament training days – IPL has consolidated its top position among all tournaments.As per an internal communication to the 10 franchises ahead of the November 24-25 mega auction, IPL 2025 is slated to begin on March 14 with the final on May 25. The start is unusually close to the 2025 Champions Trophy, which, as per the provisional schedule, is scheduled to end on March 9.
title: Longer IPL to corner quarter of cricket’s calendar

news_body: Rohit Sharma's decision to "rest" for the final Test of the Border-Gavaskar Trophy against Australia could mark the end of his Test career. India’s captain, who missed the Sydney Test, has likely played his last match for India in whites. Jasprit Bumrah stepped in as captain, revealing Rohit's absence at the toss. The selectors are set to discuss the future of both Rohit and Virat Kohli in the Test format, adding to the uncertainty surrounding their careers.
title: Rohit Sharma out of Tests, to meet Kohli for next steps.

news_body: Irfan Pathan expressed frustration with Virat Kohli after the latter got out again to a wide delivery in Sydney. Kohli, who made 17 runs, chased an outside off-stump ball from Scott Boland in the 32nd over, leading to his dismissal. Despite a reprieve earlier when Steve Smith thought he had caught Kohli’s nick, the batter failed to capitalize and fell in a similar fashion. Pathan criticized Kohli for repeating the same mistake, adding to the growing concern over his struggles with wide deliveries.
title: Irfan Pathan slams Kohli-Tendulkar comparisons, says Kohli made the same mistake.

news_body: Scott Boland has once again dismissed Virat Kohli, making it the fourth time in Sydney. Kohli’s inability to resist poking at balls outside off-stump has been exploited by Australia. In the ongoing Border-Gavaskar Trophy, Kohli was dismissed to slips again—his seventh caught-behind dismissal, all off outside-off deliveries. Boland, who has dismissed Kohli in previous matches, including the World Test Championship final, shares insights into the tactical planning that targets Kohli's weakness.
title: Scott Boland on Virat Kohli: "Once he gets in, we just..." after making him his bunny

news_body:
• Mohammad Kaif disagreed with the team management and selectors' decision to "rest" Rohit Sharma for the 5th Border-Gavaskar Trophy Test in Sydney.
• Kaif argued that despite Rohit's poor form — scoring only 31 runs in three Tests — the captain should have been included in the playing XI.
• Jasprit Bumrah took over as captain, confirming at the toss that Rohit Sharma was not part of the match.
title: BCCI Reminds Virat Kohli of His Own Struggles Amid Rohit Sharma's Drop

news_body: Rohit Sharma emphasized that the next Indian captain must earn the role, just like he, MS Dhoni, and Virat Kohli did. Speaking about his decision to sit out the Sydney Test due to poor form, Rohit stated that becoming India's captain is an honor that requires understanding its weight. He urged young players to first focus on cricket's importance and earn the responsibility through hard-fought performances over the coming years.
title: I earned India captaincy, no one served it on a plate – Rohit on Kohli, Dhoni; says next-gen not ready.

news_body: Sunil Gavaskar took a dig at Australian and English cricketers after 15 wickets fell on Day 2 of the Sydney Test between India and Australia. He pointed out that if such a collapse had happened in India, there would have been widespread criticism of the pitch. The day saw India bundle out Australia for 182, taking a slim lead of four runs. Gavaskar highlighted the contrast in reactions to pitch conditions between countries. The match is poised for a thrilling finish, with a likely conclusion on Day 3.
title: If 15 wickets fell in a day in India... – Gavaskar fires back at AUS, ENG greats!

news_body: Prasidh Krishna delivered a bold message to Sam Konstas, warning him that any aggression towards India would face a united response from the entire team. The Sydney Test between India and Australia is poised for an exciting finish, likely wrapping up by Day 3. On Day 1, Konstas stirred up Jasprit Bumrah but was dismissed by Mohammed Siraj on Day 2 as India bowled Australia out for 181, gaining a narrow four-run lead. Prasidh, with three wickets in the first innings, reminded Konstas that taking on one Indian means facing them all.
title: Prasidh’s fiery reminder to Konstas: "All eleven of us are here!" ?

news_body: Australia coach Andrew McDonald accused India of attempting to intimidate teen Sam Konstas during their celebration of Usman Khawaja’s wicket on Day 1 of the Sydney Test. McDonald expressed concern over the spat between Konstas and India’s captain, Jasprit Bumrah, after Khawaja’s dismissal. He described the way Indian players surrounded Konstas in celebration as "quite intimidating."
title: Australia coach accuses India of 'intimidating' Konstas, worries about his 'headspace'.

news_body: Virat Kohli’s ongoing love-hate relationship with the Australian crowd flared up again in Sydney. As India tried to defend 162, Kohli responded to the jeers with a bold gesture – emptying his pockets, referencing the infamous sandpapergate scandal. The taunt was a nod to the 2018 incident where Australian player Cameron Bancroft was caught using sandpaper to alter the ball during a Test against South Africa, igniting a major controversy. Kohli’s fiery response once again stirred the crowd during the Border-Gavaskar Trophy.
title: Virat Kohli taunts Aussie crowd with a cold sandpaper gesture after Smith's wicket – Watch!
